# NEW PLAN

- Refactor all of pparse so that all parsers are node driven instead of parser.current driven.

- When creating new parsers, we'll seed it with a root node that has node._value = UNLOADED.

- The NodeContext of Nodes will keep a reference to the parser until node fully parsed.
  
- When Node is fully parsed, the NodeContext will be moved to a NodeContext archive indexed by Node.
  - This NodeContext archive can become a bitbucket when low on memory.
  
- When a Node is fully parsed, _value will be set to something other than pparse.UNLOADED_VALUE.

- Node.value is a @property that will auto call Node.load() when Node._value is pparse.UNLOADED_VALUE.

- When parsing, we should favor breadth first, and when that is not reasonable it should naturally become depth first. Depth implies more effort and pparse should bias towards _lazy_.

- If we want to prepopulate the entire node tree (potentially with filtering callbacks), we can continually scan the tree for UNLOADED leaves.
  - In theory, leaves can be multi-threaded ... but performance is not a priority.

- So long as we have the original data AND top parser reference with the root node (or the root node with the parser reference), we should be able to replay parsing to any node in the tree if we decide to throw away all of the NodeContexts.
  - Node has reference to NodeContext while UNLOADED
  - (Optionally) Node keeps reference to NodeContext while _not_ UNLOADED.
    - This may be more reasonable when memory is not a constraint.
  - parser keeps mapping of Node to NodeContext in dictionary
  - parser keeps original data and root node for replays.

- Arrays of scalars elements have little to no meaning from element to element and therefore should not be Nodes.
- Scalar values have little to no meaning by themselves and therefore should not be Nodes.
- (Ordered?) Dictionaries used for sparse or named objects of fields.
- Lists used for ordered lists of arrays, vectors, lists, sets.
  - Consider a proper vector, array, set, or list in the event we allow writing.

- **Can we create a generic set of nodes with parser and state specific definitions?**
  - NodeContext would likely still be specific, but can be generated by the parser itself.
  - Node (technically all nodes should be this?), NodeMap (value is dict), NodeList (value is list)
